```{r back2, echo=FALSE}
           dcolumn = TRUE,  
           use.packages = TRUE,  
           float.pos = "h!",  
           stars = c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1),  # Customize significance stars  
           custom.model.names = c("0", "360", "374", "392",   
                                "398", "423", "431", "453", "470"),  
           digits = 2)  # Number of decimal places  
)  
  
# Print the LaTeX code  
cat(paste(latex_output3, collapse = "\n"))  

g0c <- glmer(cereal0_selected ~ Groups*dprime1 + `Cereal healthiness1`+ `Cereal healthiness2`+ `Cereal healthiness3`+ `Cereal healthiness4`+ `Cereal healthiness6`+ `Cereal healthiness7` + `Cereal healthiness8` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g360c <- glmer(cereal360_selected ~ Groups*dprime1 + `Cereal healthiness1` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g374c <- glmer(cereal374_selected ~ Groups*dprime1 + `Cereal healthiness2`+ Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g392c <- glmer(cereal392_selected ~ Groups*dprime1 + `Cereal healthiness3`+ Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


g398c <- glmer(cereal398_selected ~ Groups*dprime1 + `Cereal healthiness4` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g4231 <- glmer(cereal423_selected ~ Groups*dprime1 + `Cereal healthiness5` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


g431c <- glmer(cereal431_selected ~ Groups*dprime1 + `Cereal healthiness6` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


g453c <- glmer(cereal453_selected ~ Groups*dprime1 + `Cereal healthiness7` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g470c <- glmer(cereal470_selected ~ Groups*dprime1 + `Cereal healthiness8` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

screenreg(list(g0c, g360c, g374c, g392c, g398c, g4231, g431c, g453c, g470c))

table(merged_df$CerealSelected, merged_df$Groups)

latex_output4 <- capture.output(  
    texreg(list(g0c, g360c, g374c, g392c, g398c, g4231, g431c, g453c, g470c),  
           caption = "Glmer Regression Results n-back1",  
           label = "tab:regression",  
           booktabs = TRUE,  
           dcolumn = TRUE,  
           use.packages = TRUE,  
           float.pos = "h!",  
           stars = c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1),  # Customize significance stars  
           custom.model.names = c("0", "360", "374", "392",   
                                "398", "423", "431", "453", "470"),  
           digits = 2)  # Number of decimal places  
)  
  
# Print the LaTeX code  
cat(paste(latex_output4, collapse = "\n"))  

```

## Generalized logit mixed-effects or Multilevel Glmer - ATE, probability of selecting a product + no nback + Trial as an indicator for cereal availability + bobyqa (For publication)

```{r noback, echo=FALSE}

library(texreg)

g0d <- glmer(cereal0_selected ~ Groups + `Cereal healthiness1`+ `Cereal healthiness2`+ `Cereal healthiness3`+ `Cereal healthiness4`+ `Cereal healthiness6`+ `Cereal healthiness7` + `Cereal healthiness8` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g360d <- glmer(cereal360_selected ~ Groups + `Cereal healthiness1` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g374d <- glmer(cereal374_selected ~ Groups + `Cereal healthiness2`+ Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g392d <- glmer(cereal392_selected ~ Groups + `Cereal healthiness3`+ Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


g398d <- glmer(cereal398_selected ~ Groups + `Cereal healthiness4` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g423d <- glmer(cereal423_selected ~ Groups + `Cereal healthiness5` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


g431d <- glmer(cereal431_selected ~ Groups + `Cereal healthiness6` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


g453d <- glmer(cereal453_selected ~ Groups + `Cereal healthiness7` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g470d <- glmer(cereal470_selected ~ Groups + `Cereal healthiness8` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

screenreg(list(g0d, g360d, g374d, g392d, g398d, g423d, g431d, g453d, g470d))

latex_output <- capture.output(  
    texreg(list(g0d, g360d, g374d, g392d, g398d, g423d, g431d, g453d, g470d),  
           caption = "Glmer Regression Results 2",  
           label = "tab:regression",  
           booktabs = TRUE,  
           dcolumn = TRUE,  
           use.packages = TRUE,  
           float.pos = "h!",  
           stars = c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1),  # Customize significance stars  
           custom.model.names = c("0", "360", "374", "392",   
                                "398", "423", "431", "453", "470"),  
           digits = 2)  # Number of decimal places  
)  
  
# Print the LaTeX code  
cat(paste(latex_output, collapse = "\n"))  


```

## Generalized logit mixed-effects or Multilevel Glmer - ATE, probability of selecting a product + nback no preferences + Trial as an indicator for cereal availability + bobyqa

```{r nopref, echo=FALSE}

g0e <- glmer(cereal0_selected ~ Groups*dprime3 + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g360e <- glmer(cereal360_selected ~ Groups*dprime3  + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g374e <- glmer(cereal374_selected ~ Groups*dprime3 +  Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g392e <- glmer(cereal392_selected ~ Groups*dprime3 +  Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


g398e <- glmer(cereal398_selected ~ Groups*dprime3  + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g423e <- glmer(cereal423_selected ~ Groups*dprime3  + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


g431e <- glmer(cereal431_selected ~ Groups*dprime3 + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


g453e <- glmer(cereal453_selected ~ Groups*dprime3 + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g470e <- glmer(cereal470_selected ~ Groups*dprime3 + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

screenreg(list(g0e, g360e, g374e, g392e, g398e, g423e, g431e, g453e, g470e))

latex_output5 <- capture.output(  
    texreg(list(g0e, g360e, g374e, g392e, g398e, g423e, g431e, g453e, g470e),  
           caption = "Glmer Regression Results 2",  
           label = "tab:regression",  
           booktabs = TRUE,  
           dcolumn = TRUE,  
           use.packages = TRUE,  
           float.pos = "h!",  
           stars = c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1),  # Customize significance stars  
           custom.model.names = c("0", "360", "374", "392",   
                                "398", "423", "431", "453", "470"),  
           digits = 2)  # Number of decimal places  
)  
  
# Print the LaTeX code  
cat(paste(latex_output5, collapse = "\n")) 

g0f <- glmer(cereal0_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g360f <- glmer(cereal360_selected ~ Groups*dprime2  + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g374f <- glmer(cereal374_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 +  Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g392f <- glmer(cereal392_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 +  Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


g398f <- glmer(cereal398_selected ~ Groups*dprime2  + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g423f <- glmer(cereal423_selected ~ Groups*dprime2  + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


g431e <- glmer(cereal431_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


g453f <- glmer(cereal453_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

g470f <- glmer(cereal470_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = merged_df, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

screenreg(list(g0f, g360f, g374f, g392f, g398f, g423f, g431f, g453f, g470f))

latex_output6 <- capture.output(  
    texreg(list(g0f, g360f, g374f, g392f, g398f, g423f, g431f, g453f, g470f),  
           caption = "Glmer Regression Results 2",  
           label = "tab:regression",  
           booktabs = TRUE,  
           dcolumn = TRUE,  
           use.packages = TRUE,  
           float.pos = "h!",  
           stars = c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1),  # Customize significance stars  
           custom.model.names = c("0", "360", "374", "392",   
                                "398", "423", "431", "453", "470"),  
           digits = 2)  # Number of decimal places  
)  
  
# Print the LaTeX code  
cat(paste(latex_output6, collapse = "\n"))


```

## Multilevel log-binomial regression Lmer  - ATE, Sensitivity analysis, propensity n-back reduction size

1. What is the Propensity to Progress? The “propensity of progressing to the next level” refers to the probability that a participant advances from, say, 1-back to 2-back, or from 2-back to 3-back, given their observed characteristics (e.g., demographics, baseline cognitive ability, group assignment, etc.). This is typically estimated using a logistic regression, where the outcome is whether the participant progressed (1) or not (0). IT DID NOT WORK, FEW MISSING DATA

2. Imputation. Multiple imputation (MI) is a valid and widely accepted methodology for handling missing data in randomized controlled trials (RCTs), including for journals like BMC Public Health. In fact, MI is often recommended by CONSORT guidelines and leading statistical authorities for RCTs, especially when missing data are not negligible and the missingness mechanism is plausibly Missing At Random (MAR). 

```{r sensi1, echo=FALSE}

##MATCHED SAMPLE

complete_cases <- merged_df[!is.na(merged_df$dprime1) & !is.na(merged_df$dprime2) & !is.na(merged_df$dprime3), ]

cc1 <- lmer(as.numeric(CerealSelected) ~ Groups + (1 | `Response ID`), data = complete_cases, na.action = na.exclude)
cc2 <- lmer(as.numeric(CerealSelected) ~ Groups + Trials + (1 | `Response ID`), data = complete_cases, na.action = na.exclude)
cc3 <- lmer(as.numeric(CerealSelected) ~ Groups + Trials + `Cereal healthiness1` + `Cereal healthiness2`+ `Cereal healthiness3`+ `Cereal healthiness4`+ `Cereal healthiness6`+ `Cereal healthiness7`+ `Cereal healthiness8` + (1 | `Response ID`), data = complete_cases, na.action = na.exclude)
cc4 <- lmer(as.numeric(CerealSelected)~ Groups*dprime1  + `Cereal healthiness1` + `Cereal healthiness2`+ `Cereal healthiness3`+ `Cereal healthiness4`+ `Cereal healthiness6`+ `Cereal healthiness7`+ `Cereal healthiness8` + Trials + (1 | `Response ID`), data = complete_cases, na.action = na.exclude)
cc5 <- lmer(as.numeric(CerealSelected) ~ Groups*dprime2 + `Cereal healthiness1` + `Cereal healthiness2`+ `Cereal healthiness3`+ `Cereal healthiness4`+`Cereal healthiness6`+ `Cereal healthiness7`+ `Cereal healthiness8` + Trials + (1 | `Response ID`), data = complete_cases, na.action = na.exclude)
cc6 <- lmer(as.numeric(CerealSelected) ~ Groups*dprime3  + `Cereal healthiness1` + `Cereal healthiness2`+ `Cereal healthiness3`+ `Cereal healthiness4`+ `Cereal healthiness6`+ `Cereal healthiness7`+ `Cereal healthiness8` + Trials + (1 | `Response ID`), data = complete_cases, na.action = na.exclude)

screenreg(list(cc1,cc2,cc3,cc4,cc5,cc6))

latex_output_sensi <- capture.output(  
    texreg(list(cc1,cc2,cc3,cc4,cc5,cc6),  
           caption = "Regression Results",  
           label = "tab:regression",  
           booktabs = TRUE,  
           dcolumn = TRUE,  
           use.packages = TRUE,  
           float.pos = "h!",  
           stars = c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1),  # Customize significance stars  
           custom.model.names = c("Model 1", "Model 2", "Model 3",   
                                "Model 4", "Model 5", "Model 6"),  
           digits = 2)  # Number of decimal places  
)  
  
# Print the LaTeX code  
cat(paste(latex_output_sensi, collapse = "\n"))  


##PROPENSITY

library(dplyr)

colnames(df)[c(9)] <- c("ID")
colnames(merged_df)[c(1)] <- c("ID")

# Calculate propensity scores at participant level first
df <- df %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  slice(1) %>%  # Take first observation per participant
  mutate(
    progressed_to_2back = as.integer(!is.na(dprime2)),
    progressed_to_3back = as.integer(!is.na(dprime3))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Fit the models
model_2back <- glm(progressed_to_2back ~ Sex + Age2 + Ethnicity_reco + Level_Education2 + Groups + dprime1,
                   data = df, family = binomial)

model_3back <- glm(progressed_to_3back ~ Sex + Age2 + Ethnicity_reco + Level_Education2 + Groups + dprime2,
                   data = df, family = binomial)

screenreg(list(model_2back, model_3back))


```
## Generalized logit mixed-effects or Multilevel Glmer - ATE, probability of selecting a product + dprime2 + Trial as an indicator for cereal availability + bobyqa + sensitivity analysis


```{r sensi3, echo=FALSE}

library(dplyr)

complete_cases <- complete_cases %>%  
  mutate(cereal0_selected = ifelse(CerealSelected == 0, 1, 0)) 

gg0 <- glmer(cereal0_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + `Cereal healthiness1`+ `Cereal healthiness2`+ `Cereal healthiness3`+ `Cereal healthiness4`+ `Cereal healthiness6`+ `Cereal healthiness7` + `Cereal healthiness8`+ Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = complete_cases, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

complete_cases <- complete_cases %>%  
  mutate(cereal360_selected = ifelse(CerealSelected == 360, 1, 0)) 

gg360 <- glmer(cereal360_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + `Cereal healthiness1` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = complete_cases, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

complete_cases <- complete_cases %>%  
  mutate(cereal374_selected = ifelse(CerealSelected == 374, 1, 0)) 

gg374 <- glmer(cereal374_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + `Cereal healthiness2`+ Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = complete_cases, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


complete_cases <- complete_cases %>%  
  mutate(cereal392_selected = ifelse(CerealSelected == 392, 1, 0))  

gg392 <- glmer(cereal392_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + `Cereal healthiness3`+ Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = complete_cases, 
                na.action = na.exclude)


complete_cases <- complete_cases %>%   
  mutate(cereal398_selected = ifelse(CerealSelected == 398, 1, 0))  

gg398 <- glmer(cereal398_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + `Cereal healthiness4` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = complete_cases, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

complete_cases <- complete_cases %>%  
  mutate(cereal423_selected = ifelse(CerealSelected == 423, 1, 0))  

gg423 <- glmer(cereal423_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + `Cereal healthiness5` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = complete_cases, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

complete_cases <- complete_cases %>%   
  mutate(cereal431_selected = ifelse(CerealSelected == 431, 1, 0))  

gg431 <- glmer(cereal431_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + `Cereal healthiness6` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = complete_cases, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

complete_cases <- complete_cases %>%  
  mutate(cereal453_selected = ifelse(CerealSelected == 453, 1, 0))  

gg453 <- glmer(cereal453_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + `Cereal healthiness7` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = complete_cases, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

complete_cases <- complete_cases %>%  
  mutate(cereal470_selected = ifelse(CerealSelected == 470, 1, 0))  

gg470 <- glmer(cereal470_selected ~ Groups*dprime2 + `Cereal healthiness8` + Trials +  (1 | `Response ID`),
                family = binomial("logit"),
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",  
                                     optCtrl = list(maxfun = 200000)),
                data = complete_cases, 
                na.action = na.exclude)

screenreg(list(gg0, gg360, gg374, gg392, gg398, gg423, gg431, gg453, gg470))

latex_outputsensi_2back <- capture.output(  
    texreg(list(gg0, gg360, gg374, gg392, gg398, gg423, gg431, gg453, gg470),  
           caption = "Glmer Regression Results",  
           label = "tab:regression",  
           booktabs = TRUE,  
           dcolumn = TRUE,  
           use.packages = TRUE,  
           float.pos = "h!",  
           stars = c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1),  # Customize significance stars  
           custom.model.names = c("0", "360", "374", "392",   
                                "398", "423", "431", "453", "470"),  
           digits = 2)  # Number of decimal places  
)  
  
# Print the LaTeX code  
cat(paste(latex_outputsensi_2back, collapse = "\n"))  


```



## statistics for publication

```{r stat1, echo=FALSE}

dprime3_stats <- aggregate(dprime3 ~ Groups, data = df, 
                         FUN = function(x) c(mean = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), 
                                           sd = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)))
print(dprime3_stats)

cross_tab4 <- table(df$`Are there children in your household?`, df$Groups)

# Add percentages to the table
cross_tab4_percent <- prop.table(cross_tab4, margin = 2) * 100
print(cross_tab4_percent)

cross_tab5 <- table(df$`Are you responsible for household purchases?`, df$Groups)
print(cross_tab5)

# Add percentages to the table
cross_tab5_percent <- prop.table(cross_tab5, margin = 2) * 100
print(cross_tab5_percent)

cross_tab6 <- table(df$FamiliarityBrands, df$Groups)
print(cross_tab6)

# Add percentages to the table
cross_tab6_percent <- prop.table(cross_tab6, margin = 2) * 100
print(cross_tab6_percent)

cross_tab7 <- table(df$`On a scale of 1 to 5, how would you rate the amount of information you had to choose cereals according to your nutritional preferences? Where 5 means information overload and 1 means insufficient information.`, df$Groups)
print(cross_tab7)

# Add percentages to the table
cross_tab7_percent <- prop.table(cross_tab7, margin = 2) * 100
print(cross_tab7_percent)

cross_tab8 <- table(df$`On a scale of 1 to 5, how much did you notice the labels on the cereal packages during the experiment? Where 5 means always and 1 means never....69`, df$Groups)
print(cross_tab8)

# Add percentages to the table
cross_tab8_percent <- prop.table(cross_tab8, margin = 2) * 100
print(cross_tab8_percent)

cross_tab9 <- table(df$`On a scale of 1 to 5, how much did you notice the labels on the cereal packages during the experiment? Where 5 means always and 1 means never....88`, df$Groups)
print(cross_tab9)

# Add percentages to the table
cross_tab9_percent <- prop.table(cross_tab9, margin = 2) * 100
print(cross_tab9_percent)

cross_tab10 <- table(df$Income2, df$Groups)
print(cross_tab10)

# Add percentages to the table
cross_tab10_percent <- prop.table(cross_tab10, margin = 2) * 100
print(cross_tab10_percent)

cross_tab11 <- table(df$`Are you trying to lose weight?`, df$Groups)
print(cross_tab11)

# Add percentages to the table
cross_tab11_percent <- prop.table(cross_tab11, margin = 2) * 100
print(cross_tab11_percent)

cross_tab12 <- table(df$Check_Ingredients, df$Groups)
print(cross_tab12)

cross_tab12_percent <- prop.table(cross_tab12, margin = 2) * 100
print(cross_tab12_percent)

#Ranking preferences


summary_ranking1 <- aggregate(`Cereal healthiness1` ~ Groups, data = df, 
                              FUN = function(x) c(Mean = mean(x), SD = sd(x)))

summary_ranking2 <- aggregate(`Rank the 8 cereal packages from most preferred to least preferred by dragging the products to change their order of position. The blue number on the left side indicates the position of the product in the list. - IM_cUa2Sgw1Z4KERE2` ~ Groups, data = df, 
                              FUN = function(x) c(Mean = mean(x), SD = sd(x)))

summary_ranking3 <- aggregate(df$`Rank the 8 cereal packages from most preferred to least preferred by dragging the products to change their order of position. The blue number on the left side indicates the position of the product in the list. - IM_lnH5RoeqxamC6re` ~ Groups, data = df, 
                              FUN = function(x) c(Mean = mean(x), SD = sd(x)))

summary_ranking4 <- aggregate(df$`Rank the 8 cereal packages from most preferred to least preferred by dragging the products to change their order of position. The blue number on the left side indicates the position of the product in the list. - IM_882CtF42zwvUZv0` ~ Groups, data = df, 
                              FUN = function(x) c(Mean = mean(x), SD = sd(x)))

summary_ranking5 <- aggregate(df$`Rank the 8 cereal packages from most preferred to least preferred by dragging the products to change their order of position. The blue number on the left side indicates the position of the product in the list. - IM_dgaZUlSdxdpoDbM` ~ Groups, data = df, 
                              FUN = function(x) c(Mean = mean(x), SD = sd(x)))

summary_ranking6 <- aggregate(df$`Rank the 8 cereal packages from most preferred to least preferred by dragging the products to change their order of position. The blue number on the left side indicates the position of the product in the list. - IM_3r4Nrp3TqC6BOlg` ~ Groups, data = df, 
                              FUN = function(x) c(Mean = mean(x), SD = sd(x)))

summary_ranking7 <- aggregate(df$`Rank the 8 cereal packages from most preferred to least preferred by dragging the products to change their order of position. The blue number on the left side indicates the position of the product in the list. - IM_6DzLXmUBf30NEXA` ~ Groups, data = df, 
                              FUN = function(x) c(Mean = mean(x), SD = sd(x)))

summary_ranking8 <- aggregate(df$`Cereal healthiness8` ~ Groups, data = df, 
                              FUN = function(x) c(Mean = mean(x), SD = sd(x)))

mean(df$`Cereal healthiness1`)

mean(df$`Rank the 8 cereal packages from most preferred to least preferred by dragging the products to change their order of position. The blue number on the left side indicates the position of the product in the list. - IM_cUa2Sgw1Z4KERE2`)

mean(df$`Rank the 8 cereal packages from most preferred to least preferred by dragging the products to change their order of position. The blue number on the left side indicates the position of the product in the list. - IM_lnH5RoeqxamC6re`)

mean(df$`Rank the 8 cereal packages from most preferred to least preferred by dragging the products to change their order of position. The blue number on the left side indicates the position of the product in the list. - IM_882CtF42zwvUZv0`)

mean(df$`Rank the 8 cereal packages from most preferred to least preferred by dragging the products to change their order of position. The blue number on the left side indicates the position of the product in the list. - IM_dgaZUlSdxdpoDbM`)

mean(df$`Rank the 8 cereal packages from most preferred to least preferred by dragging the products to change their order of position. The blue number on the left side indicates the position of the product in the list. - IM_3r4Nrp3TqC6BOlg`)

mean(df$`Rank the 8 cereal packages from most preferred to least preferred by dragging the products to change their order of position. The blue number on the left side indicates the position of the product in the list. - IM_6DzLXmUBf30NEXA`)

mean(df$`Cereal healthiness8`)

cross_tab13 <- table(df$Check_Ingredients, df$Groups)
print(cross_tab12)

cross_tab12_percent <- prop.table(cross_tab12, margin = 2) * 100
print(cross_tab12_percent)




```
